{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

## Odds and log-odds {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Odds** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-odds))
$$\odds \eqdef \frac{\Pr(A)}{\Pr(\neg A)}$$

**Conditional odds** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-c-odds))
$$\odds(A|B) \eqdef \frac{\Pr(A|B)}{\Pr(\neg A|B)}$$

**Odds function** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-odds-fn))
$$\oddsf{\pi} \eqdef \frac{\pi}{1-\pi}$$

**Probability to odds** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-prob-to-odds), [cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-oddsf-to-odds))
$$\odds = \frac{\prob}{1-\prob} = \oddsf{\prob}$$

**Simplified odds expressions; odds of a non-event** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-odds-simplified), [cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-odds-neg))
$$\oddsf{\pi} = \frac{1}{\pi^{-1}-1} = \invf{\pi^{-1}-1},
\qquad \odds(\neg A) = \frac{1-\pi}{\pi} = \pi^{-1}-1$$

**Odds ratio** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-OR))
$$\ratio(\odds_1, \odds_2) \eqdef \frac{\odds_1}{\odds_2}$$

**OR as ratio of probability ratios** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-or-ratio-ratio))
$$\ratio(\odds_1, \odds_2)
= \frac{\odds_1}{\odds_2}
= \frac{\paren{\frac{\pi_1}{1-\pi_1}}}{\paren{\frac{\pi_2}{1-\pi_2}}}$$

**Odds ratios are reversible, marginally and conditionally** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-or-swap), [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-conditional-OR-swap))
$$\ror(A|B) = \ror(B|A),
\qquad \ror(A|B,C) = \ror(B|A,C)$$

:::

## Inverse-odds and probability recovery {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Odds to probability** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-odds-to-prob))
$$\pi = \frac{\odds}{1+\odds}$$

**Inverse-odds function** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-inv-odds); recovers probability by [cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-invodds-pi))
$$\invoddsf{\odds} \eqdef \frac{\odds}{1 + \odds},
\qquad \prob = \invoddsf{\odds}$$

**Simplified inverse-odds** ([lem](logistic-regression.qmd#lem-invodds-simplified))
$$\invoddsf{\odds} = \frac{1}{1+\odds^{-1}} = (1+\odds^{-1})^{-1}$$

**One minus inverse-odds, and its complement** ([lem](logistic-regression.qmd#lem-one-minus-odds-inv), [cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-inverse-odds-nonevent))
$$1 - \prob = \frac{1}{1+\odds},
\qquad 1+\odds = \frac{1}{1-\prob}$$

:::

## Log-odds (logit) and expit {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Log-odds, and log-odds from probability** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-logodds), [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-logodds-pi))
$$\logodds \eqdef \logf{\omega},
\qquad \logodds = \logf{\frac{\prob}{1-\prob}}$$

**Logit function, and expanded form** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-logit-fn), [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-logit-function))
$$\logit(\prob) \eqdef \logf{\oddsf{\prob}} = \logf{\frac{\prob}{1-\prob}}$$

**Log-odds equals logit** ([cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-logodds-logit))
$$\logodds = \logitf{\prob}$$

**Odds and probability from log-odds** ([lem](logistic-regression.qmd#lem-odds-from-logodds), [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-prob-from-logodds))
$$\odds = \expf{\logodds},
\qquad \prob = \frac{\expf{\eta}}{1+\expf{\eta}}$$

**Expit / inverse-logit function, and equivalent expressions** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-expit), [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-expit-expressions))
$$\expit(\logodds) \eqdef \invoddsf{\expf{\logodds}}
= \frac{\exp{\logodds}}{1+\exp{\logodds}} = (1 + \exp{-\logodds})^{-1}$$

**Probability as expit** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-expit-prob-logodds))
$$\prob = \expitf{\logodds}$$ {#eq-prob-as-expit}

**Logit and expit are inverses** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-logit-expit))
$$\logitf{\expitf{\logodds}} = \logodds \qquad \expitf{\logitf{\pi}} = \pi$$

:::

::: {#fig-expit-logit-diagram-summary}

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_fig-expit-logit-diagram-content.qmd >}}

Diagram of logistic regression link and inverse link functions

:::

## Rare events {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Odds minus probability** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-odds-minus-probs))
$$\odds - \pi = \frac{\pi^2}{1-\pi}, \quad \text{where } \odds = \frac{\pi}{1-\pi}$$

**OR as RR times a correction factor** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-rare-disease-or-rr))
$$\ratio(\odds_1, \odds_2)
= \rho(\pi_1, \pi_2) \cdot \frac{1-\pi_2}{1-\pi_1}$$
When $\pi_1$ and $\pi_2$ are both small,
the correction factor is close to 1,
so the odds ratio is close to the risk ratio.

:::

## Derivatives {.unnumbered}

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_eq-logistic-deriv-matrix.qmd >}}

Column labels indicate the numerators of the derivatives;
row labels indicate the denominators
(dimensions and details in [tbl](logistic-regression.qmd#tbl-logistic-deriv-matrix)).

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Derivative of expit** ([cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-deriv-expit))
$$\dexpitf{\logodds} = \expitf{\logodds}(1 - \expitf{\logodds})$$

:::

## Logistic regression model {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Logistic regression** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-logistic-regression))
$$Y_i|\vX_i \simind \Ber(\pi(\vX_i)), \qquad \logitf{\pi(\vx)} = \tp{\vx}\vb$$

:::

Throughout this summary,
$\vb = (\b_0, \b_1, \ldots, \b_p)$ is the $(p+1) \times 1$ coefficient vector (including the intercept),
$\mX$ is the $n \times (p+1)$ design matrix whose $i^{\text{th}}$ row is $\tp{\vx_i}$,
and $\veps$ is the $n \times 1$ error vector with components $\eps_i \eqdef y_i - \pi_i$.

## Log-likelihood and score function {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Log-likelihood component** ([lem](logistic-regression.qmd#lem-logistic-loglik-component))
$$\ell_i(\pi_i) = y_i \eta_i - \logf{1+\odds_i}$$

**Score function as sum of components** ([lem](logistic-regression.qmd#lem-score-logistic), [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-logistic-score-comp))
$$\vec{\llik'}(\vb) = \sumin \vec{\llik'_i}(\vb),
\qquad \llik_i'(\vb) = \vx_i \eps_i$$

:::

**Score function** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-logistic-score-fn))
$$\vec{\llik'}(\vb) = \sumin \vx_i\eps_i
= \underbrace{\tp{\mX}}_{(p+1) \times n}\underbrace{\veps}_{n \times 1}$$

## Maximum likelihood estimation {.unnumbered}

**Estimating equation** ([eq](logistic-regression.qmd#eq-score-logistic))
The MLE $\hvb$ solves the score equation:
$$\underbrace{\vec{\llik'}(\hvb)}_{(p+1) \times 1} = \sumin \underbrace{\vx_i}_{(p+1) \times 1}\underbrace{\paren{y_i - \expitf{\tp{\vx_i}\hvb}}}_{1 \times 1} = \mathbf{0}_{(p+1) \times 1}$$

This estimating equation has no closed-form solution in general;
$\hvb$ must be computed by numerical iteration.

**Hessian** ([eq](logistic-regression.qmd#eq-logistic-hess))
$$\underbrace{\llik''(\vb)}_{(p+1) \times (p+1)} = -\underbrace{\tp{\mX}}_{(p+1) \times n} \underbrace{\matr{D}}_{n \times n} \underbrace{\mX}_{n \times (p+1)}, \qquad \matr{D} \eqdef \text{diag}(\Varf{Y_i|\vX_i=\vx_i}) = \text{diag}(\pi_i(1-\pi_i))$$

**Concavity** ([rem](logistic-regression.qmd#rem-hessian-nsd)):
the Hessian is negative semi-definite for every $\vb$
(negative definite when $\mX$ has full column rank),
so the log-likelihood is concave
and every solution of the score equation is a global maximum.

**Newton-Raphson update** ([Newton-Raphson method](intro-MLEs.qmd#sec-newton-raphson))
$$\underbrace{\esttmp{\vb}}_{(p+1) \times 1} \leftarrow \underbrace{\esttmp{\vb}}_{(p+1) \times 1} - \underbrace{\inv{\hessf{\vec y; \esttmp{\vb}}}}_{(p+1) \times (p+1)} \underbrace{\scoref{\vec y; \esttmp{\vb}}}_{(p+1) \times 1}$$

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**One-sample MLE for odds** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-est-odds))
$$\hat{\odds}= \frac{x}{n-x}$$

:::

## Wald tests and confidence intervals for coefficients {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Wald test statistic** for $H_0: \b_k = \b_{k,0}$
([CLT for MLEs](intro-MLEs.qmd#thm-dist-mle))
$$z_k = \frac{\hb_k - \b_{k,0}}{\HSE{\hb_k}},
\qquad z_k \dsim N(0,1) \text{ under } H_0$$

**95% confidence intervals for $\b_k$ and $e^{\b_k}$** (MLE invariance)
$$\hb_k \pm 1.96 \cdot \HSE{\hb_k},
\qquad e^{\b_k} \in \paren{e^{\hb_k - 1.96 \cdot \HSE{\hb_k}},\; e^{\hb_k + 1.96 \cdot \HSE{\hb_k}}}$$

For $k \neq 0$, $e^{\b_k}$ is an odds ratio;
for $k = 0$, $e^{\b_0}$ is the baseline odds.

:::

## Odds ratios in logistic regression {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**General OR formula** ([lem](logistic-regression.qmd#lem-logistic-OR))
$$\ror(\vx,\vxs) = \expf{\logodds(\vx) - \logodds(\vxs)}$$

**Difference in log-odds; OR in terms of $\difflogodds$** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-difflogodds), [cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-logistic-OR))
$$\difflogodds \eqdef \logodds(\vx) - \logodds(\vxs),
\qquad \ror(\vx,\vxs) = \expf{\difflogodds}$$

**$\difflogodds$ from covariates** ([lem](logistic-regression.qmd#lem-diff-logodds))
$$\difflogodds = (\vx - \vxs)\cdot\vb$$

**Difference in covariate patterns; $\difflogodds$ from $\diffvx$** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-diff-covs), [cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-diff-logodds))
$$\diffvx \eqdef \vx - \vxs,
\qquad \difflogodds = \diffvx \cdot \vb$$

**OR in terms of $\diffvx$** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-logistic-OR))
$$\ror(\vx,\vxs) = \expf{(\diffvx) \cdot \vb}$$

**Log OR equals $\difflogodds$** ([cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-log-or))
$$\logf{\ror(\vx,\vxs)} = \difflogodds$$

:::

## Inference for log-odds and odds ratios {.unnumbered}

**Estimated SE of log-odds** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-est-se-logodds))
$$\hVar{\hat\logodds(\vx)} = \underbrace{\tp{\vx}}_{1 \times (p+1)}\underbrace{\eSigma}_{(p+1) \times (p+1)}\underbrace{\vx}_{(p+1) \times 1},
\qquad \HSE{\hat\logodds(\vx)} = \sqrt{\tp{\vx}\eSigma\vx}$$

**Estimated SE of $\Delta\widehat{\logodds}$** ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-est-se-diff-logodds))
$$\hVar{\Delta{\hat\logodds}} = \underbrace{\tp{\diffvx}}_{1 \times (p+1)}\underbrace{\eSigma}_{(p+1) \times (p+1)}\underbrace{(\diffvx)}_{(p+1) \times 1},
\qquad \HSE{\Delta{\hat\logodds}} = \sqrt{\tp{\diffvx}\eSigma(\diffvx)}$$

**CI for an odds ratio** ([eq](logistic-regression.qmd#eq-ci-OR-exp); details in [sec](logistic-regression.qmd#sec-or-inference)):
exponentiate the endpoints of the CI for $\difflogodds$:
$$\ror(\vx,\vxs) \in (e^L, e^R),
\qquad (L, R) = \hdifflogodds \mp 1.96 \cdot \HSE{\hdifflogodds}$$

## Odds ratios in study designs {.unnumbered}

**Disease and exposure odds ratios** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-disease-OR), [def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-exposure-OR))
$$\ror(D|E) \eqdef \frac{\odds(D|E)}{\odds(D|\neg E)},
\qquad \ror(E|D) \eqdef \frac{\odds(E|D)}{\odds(E|\neg D)}$$

**Case-control studies** ([sec](logistic-regression.qmd#sec-CC-studies)):
outcome-stratified sampling breaks estimation of risks, risk ratios,
and the components of the disease odds ratio ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-disease-OR)),
but the exposure odds ratio ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-exposure-OR)) is estimable,
and it equals the disease odds ratio by reversibility ([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-or-swap)).

**2×2 table shortcut** ([tbl](logistic-regression.qmd#tbl-2x2-generic); cell counts $a, b$ = exposed events and non-events, $c, d$ = unexposed events and non-events):
$$\hodds(Event|Exposed) = \frac{a}{b},
\qquad \hodds(Event|\neg Exposed) = \frac{c}{d},
\qquad \ror(Exposed, \neg Exposed) = \frac{ad}{bc}$$

## Model fit and comparison {.unnumbered}

Details in [sec](logistic-regression.qmd#sec-gof);
here $q$ is the number of distinct covariate patterns,
$p$ is the number of $\b$ parameters,
and $\ell_{\text{full}}$ is the saturated-model log-likelihood.

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Deviance test** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-deviance-test))
$$D \eqdef 2(\ell_{\text{full}} - \ell(\hb; \mathbf x)),
\qquad D \dsim \chi^2(q - p)$$

**Hosmer-Lemeshow test** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-hosmer-lemeshow); $g$ groups by predicted probability)
$$X^2 \eqdef \sum_{c=1}^g \frac{(o_c - e_c)^2}{e_c} \dsim \chi^2(g-2)$$

**Pearson chi-squared GOF test** (sum of squared Pearson residuals, [def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-pearson-residual))
$$X^2 = \sum_{k=1}^q X_k^2 \dsim \chi^2(q-p)$$

**AIC and BIC** [lower is better]
([AIC](Linear-models-overview.qmd#def-aic) [@akaike1974new];
[BIC](Linear-models-overview.qmd#def-bic) [@schwarz1978estimating])
$$\text{AIC} = -2 \ell(\hth) + 2p,
\qquad \text{BIC} = -2 \ell(\hth) + p \logf{n}$$

**Likelihood ratio test** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-lrt); for nested models with $p_0 < p_1$ parameters —
here $\ell_1$ and $\ell_0$ are the two fitted models' log-likelihoods,
not the saturated model's)
$$\Lambda \eqdef 2(\ell_1(\hth_1) - \ell_0(\hth_0)),
\qquad \Lambda \dsim \chi^2(p_1 - p_0) \text{ under } H_0$$
by [Wilks' theorem](intro-MLEs.qmd#thm-wilks).

:::

## Residual-based diagnostics {.unnumbered}

Residuals are computed per covariate pattern $k$ (grouped data);
$h_k$ denotes the leverage of pattern $k$ ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-leverage-glm)).

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Response residual** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-response-residual-logistic))
$$e_k \eqdef \bar y_k - \hat{\pi}(x_k)$$

**Pearson residual (and standardized version)** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-pearson-residual), [def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-standardized-pearson-residual))
$$X_k \eqdef \frac{\bar y_k - \hat\pi_k}{\sqrt{\hat \pi_k (1-\hat\pi_k)/n_k}},
\qquad r_{P_k} \eqdef \frac{X_k}{\sqrt{1-h_k}}$$

**Deviance residual (and standardized version)** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-deviance-residual-logistic))
$$d_k \eqdef \signt(y_k - n_k \hat \pi_k)\left\{\sqrt{2[\ell_{\text{full}}(x_k) - \ell(\hb; x_k)]}\right\},
\qquad r_{D_k} \eqdef \frac{d_k}{\sqrt{1-h_k}}$$

**Cook's distance** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-cooks-distance-glm))
$$D_k \eqdef \frac{(r_{P_k})^2}{p} \cdot \frac{h_k}{1 - h_k}$$

:::

**Leverage** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-leverage-glm)):
$h_k$ is the $k$-th diagonal element of the weighted hat matrix
(with $\mX$ here the $q \times p$ design matrix of covariate patterns):
$$\underbrace{\matr{H}}_{q \times q}
\eqdef
\underbrace{\matr{W}^{1/2}}_{q \times q}
\underbrace{\mX}_{q \times p}
\paren{\underbrace{\tp{\mX}\,\matr{W}\,\mX}_{p \times p}}^{-1}
\underbrace{\tp{\mX}}_{p \times q}
\underbrace{\matr{W}^{1/2}}_{q \times q},
\qquad \underbrace{\matr{W}}_{q \times q} \eqdef \text{diag}\paren{n_k \, \hat\pi_k \, (1 - \hat\pi_k)}$$

## Comparing probabilities {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Risk difference** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-RD))
$$\delta(\pi_1,\pi_2) \eqdef \pi_1 - \pi_2$$

**Risk ratio** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-RR))
$$\rho(\pi_1,\pi_2) \eqdef \frac{\pi_1}{\pi_2}$$

**Relative risk difference** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-RRD); equals RR minus 1 by [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-rrd-vs-rr))
$$\xi(\pi_1,\pi_2) \eqdef \frac{\delta(\pi_1,\pi_2)}{\pi_2} = \rho(\pi_1,\pi_2) - 1$$

:::

## Marginal risks from logistic regression {.unnumbered}

:::{style="column-count: 2; column-gap: 2em;"}

**Conditional predicted risk** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-cond-predicted-risk))
$$\pi(a, z) \eqdef \Pr(Y = 1 \mid A = a, Z = z)$$

**Conditional RD and RR** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-cond-rd), [def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-cond-rrr))
$$\text{RD}(z) \eqdef \pi(1, z) - \pi(0, z),
\qquad \text{RR}(z) \eqdef \frac{\pi(1, z)}{\pi(0, z)}$$

**Observed marginal risk** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-observed-marginal-risk); standardization form by [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-observed-marginal-risk))
$$\pi(a) \eqdef \Pr(Y = 1 \mid A = a) = \E{\pi(a, Z) \mid A = a}$$

**Potential mean and causal marginal risk** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-potential-mean), [def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-causal-marginal-risk); identification form by [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-causal-marginal-risk))
$$\mu_a \eqdef \E{Y^a},
\qquad \pi_a \eqdef \Pr(Y^a = 1) = \E{\pi(a, Z)}$$

**No confounding: observed = causal** ([cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-observed-equals-causal); the two estimands are contrasted in [rem](logistic-regression.qmd#rem-observed-vs-causal-marginal-risk))
$$Z \ind A \implies \pi(a) = \pi_a$$

**G-computation estimator** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-g-computation); consistent for $\pi_a$ by [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-g-computation-consistency))
$$\hat\pi_a \eqdef \frac{1}{n}\sumin \hat\pi_{a,i},
\qquad \hat\pi_{a,i} \eqdef \hat\pi(a, z_i)$$

**Predictive margins** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-predictive-margins); full-sample form [def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-full-sample-predictive-margin) is consistent for $\pi_a$ by [cor](logistic-regression.qmd#cor-full-sample-pm))
$$\widehat{\text{PM}}(a \mid S) \eqdef \frac{1}{|S|} \sum_{i \in S} \hat\pi_{a,i},
\qquad \widehat{\text{PM}}(a) \eqdef \frac{1}{n}\sumin \hat\pi_{a,i} = \hat\pi_a$$

**Exposed-subgroup predictive margin** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-subgroup-predictive-margin); consistent for $\pi(a)$ by [thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-subgroup-pm); equals $\bar Y_a$ exactly by [lem](logistic-regression.qmd#lem-subgroup-pm-proportion))
$$\hat\pi_a(a) \eqdef \frac{1}{n_a} \sum_{i : A_i = a} \hat\pi_{a,i} = \bar Y_a$$

**Observed marginal RD and RR** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-observed-marginal-rd), [def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-observed-marginal-rr))
$$\text{OMRD} \eqdef \pi(1) - \pi(0),
\qquad \text{OMRR} \eqdef \frac{\pi(1)}{\pi(0)}$$

**Causal marginal RD and RR** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-causal-marginal-rd), [def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-causal-marginal-rr))
$$\text{CMRD} \eqdef \pi_1 - \pi_0,
\qquad \text{CMRR} \eqdef \frac{\pi_1}{\pi_0}$$

:::

**Bootstrap SE and CI for marginal contrasts**
([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-bootstrap-marginal-rd); see [Bootstrap Confidence Intervals](basic-statistical-methods.qmd#sec-bootstrap-ci) and [exm](logistic-regression.qmd#exm-wcgs-marginal-rd)):
resample the data with replacement $B$ times;
refit the logistic model and recompute the marginal contrast on each resample;
the bootstrap SE is the standard deviation of the $B$ estimates,
and a 95% percentile CI uses their 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles.

**Collapsibility** ([def](logistic-regression.qmd#def-collapsibility)):
an estimand $\theta$ is collapsible with respect to $Z$ if
$$\theta \eqdef \E{\theta(Z)}$$
Risk differences are collapsible;
risk ratios and odds ratios are not collapsible in general
([thm](logistic-regression.qmd#thm-collapsibility); counterexample in [exm](logistic-regression.qmd#exm-collapsibility)).
